paper_assessments.experimental_system: Non-human (mouse) and human NK cells, CAR-NK; not T cells, no TCR
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: CREM deletion enhances CAR-NK cell effector function both in vitro and in vivo
paper_assessments.rationale: Direct causal checkpoint of effector function, but in NK cells lacking a TCR, so not a T cell TCR phenotype.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: Non-human (mouse) CREMα-transgenic primary CD4 T cells; SLE relevance in humans
paper_assessments.phenotype: proximal TCR signalling inhibition or attenuation
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: enhanced binding of CREMα to the CD3ζ chain promoter resulting in decreased CD3ζ chain expression
paper_assessments.rationale: CREMα directly represses CD3ζ (proximal TCR component) and IL-2, mechanistically linking CREM to TCR attenuation, though net signalling is paradoxically enhanced.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: CrEM is a physician-led subspecialty focused on rapid, high-acuity care
paper_assessments.rationale: Acronym collision: Critical Emergency Medicine, unrelated to the CREM transcription factor or T cells.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: CREM is a negative regulator of the TH2 response
paper_assessments.rationale: Perturbational but concerns TH2 cytokine differentiation, not TCR inhibition or a proximal inhibitory phenotype.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: CREM disruption contributes to male infertility in type 2 diabetes
paper_assessments.rationale: Review of CREM in testicular dysfunction; non-immune system, no T cell or TCR relevance.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: Mouse and human CD4 T cells; Th17 subset
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: ICER is essential for Th17 differentiation and promotes autoimmune diseases
paper_assessments.rationale: ICER/CREM drives pro-inflammatory Th17 differentiation; lineage specification, not TCR inhibition.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: CREM variants are linked to increased susceptibility to undernutrition and diarrheal disease
paper_assessments.rationale: Genetic association and epithelial Crem deletion; no TCR or inhibitory T cell phenotype examined.

paper_assessments.rationale: Concerns CREB, not CREM, and addresses Th17/Treg differentiation rather than TCR inhibition.

paper_assessments.rationale: Case report of an EWSR1::CREM fusion tumor; no T cell or TCR signalling content.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: CREM is highly expressed in synovial fluid T cells and contributes to cytokine expression
paper_assessments.rationale: CREM drives an inflammatory effector T cell phenotype; pro-inflammatory, not TCR inhibition.

parsed.tier_rationale: Only one primary T cell study (PMC5165720) materially links CREM to proximal TCR machinery, showing direct CREMα binding and repression of the CD3ζ chain plus negative IL-2 regulation. This provides indirect mechanistic evidence connecting CREM to attenuation of a proximal TCR component, but the functional outcome is paradoxically enhanced signalling, so directness toward TCR inhibition is incomplete. The NK checkpoint study (PMC12286855) gives clean perturbational evidence but is a non-T-cell, non-TCR system. Remaining CREM T cell papers address TH2/Th17 differentiation or inflammatory effector phenotypes, which are insufficient for moderate. No primary study delivers clean direct causal TCR inhibition with an inhibitory functional readout, so strong is not met; the mechanistic CD3ζ link exceeds weak. Applying the lower-tier rule where ambiguous.
